title: NOC 21201 – Landscape Architects | GTR Immigration
description: Landscape Architects play a critical role in shaping public parks, residential communities, commercial developments, waterfronts, and sustainable urban spaces across Canada. Classified under NOC 21201 (TEER 1), this profession qualifies for multiple skilled-immigration streams, including Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), employer-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2120 – Architects, urban planners, and land surveyors

duties: Consult with clients to determine site and design needs
Survey and assess land conditions and surroundings
Design outdoor spaces considering climate, structures, and usage
Prepare detailed drawings for gardens, walkways, patios, lighting, and water features
Produce grading, drainage, and planting plans
Estimate project costs and evaluate tenders
Participate in community and urban-design master planning
employment_requirements: Master’s degree may be required for senior roles
Provincial registration required in Ontario, Alberta, and British Columbia
In other provinces: two years of experience + provincial-association interview
job_outlook: Between 2022–2031, Canada is projected to create 2,700 new Landscape-Architect positions, with roughly 2,700 job seekers—indicating a balanced but competitive immigration environment, particularly for internationally trained professionals who secure provincial licensing and employer support.
additional_information: Landscape Architects remain in demand nationwide due to:
Urban redevelopment projectsSustainable infrastructure planningPublic-space revitalizationClimate-resilient land designGovernment environmental initiatives
Government development agenciesLandscape-design consultanciesArchitectural & engineering firmsEnvironmental planning companiesSelf-employed design studios
Annual salaries typically range between $52,800–$181,300 CAD, depending on licensing, province, seniority, and specialization.
Why Choose Canada as a Landscape Architect?

Growing infrastructure and green-space investmentProvincial nomination optionsStrong wage growth for senior designersPR-linked skilled-worker pathwaysUniversal healthcareFamily sponsorship programsMulticultural professional environment
